questions.question: Where is the library?
questions.answer: The Central Library is located in Building A, first floor. It's open 24/7 during exam periods.

questions.question: How do I get to the student center?
questions.answer: The Student Center is in Building C, right across from the main entrance. Look for the blue signs.

questions.question: Where can I find parking?
questions.answer: Student parking is available in Lots P1-P5. P1 and P2 are closest to academic buildings. Parking permits required.

questions.question: Where is the registrar's office?
questions.answer: The Registrar's Office is in the Administration Building, Room 205. Hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM.

questions.question: Where can I eat on campus?
questions.answer: Main dining options: Central Dining Hall (Building D), Campus Café (Student Center), and Food Court (Library basement).

questions.question: Where is the gym?
questions.answer: The Fitness Center is in the Recreation Building, accessible with your student ID. Pool, weight room, and courts available.

questions.question: Where is the health center?
questions.answer: The Health & Wellness Center is in Building F, first floor. Emergency services available 24/7.

questions.question: Where can I study?
questions.answer: Study spaces: Library (quiet zones), Student Center (group study), Academic buildings (study lounges), and outdoor study areas.

questions.question: Where is the bookstore?
questions.answer: The Campus Bookstore is on the ground floor of the Student Center. They sell textbooks, supplies, and campus merchandise.

questions.question: Where are the dorms?
questions.answer: Residence halls are located on the north side of campus: Harrison Hall, Madison Hall, and Jefferson Hall.

questions.question: What time does the library close?
questions.answer: Library hours: Mon-Thu 7:00 AM - 2:00 AM, Fri 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM - 2:00 AM.

questions.question: When is the cafeteria open?
questions.answer: Central Dining Hall: Breakfast 7:00-10:00 AM, Lunch 11:00 AM-3:00 PM, Dinner 5:00-9:00 PM daily.

questions.question: What are the gym hours?
questions.answer: Fitness Center: Mon-Fri 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sat-Sun 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM. Pool closes 30 minutes earlier.

questions.question: When is the bookstore open?
questions.answer: Bookstore hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Closed Sundays.

questions.question: What time does the health center close?
questions.answer: Health Center: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM. Emergency services available 24/7.

questions.question: When is the registrar open?
questions.answer: Registrar's Office: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM. Extended hours during registration periods.

questions.question: What are the student center hours?
questions.answer: Student Center: Mon-Thu 6:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Fri-Sat 6:00 AM - 1:00 AM, Sun 8:00 AM - 12:00 AM.

questions.question: When does the computer lab close?
questions.answer: Computer labs: Available 24/7 in the library. Other labs: Mon-Fri 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, weekends 9:00 AM - 9:00 PM.

questions.question: Who do I contact for IT support?
questions.answer: IT Help Desk: (555) 123-4567, help@campus.edu. Located in Library basement. Hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM.

questions.question: What's the admissions phone number?
questions.answer: Admissions Office: (555) 123-4568, admissions@campus.edu. Located in Administration Building, Room 101.

questions.question: Who handles financial aid?
questions.answer: Financial Aid Office: (555) 123-4569, finaid@campus.edu. Administration Building, Room 150. Walk-ins welcome.

questions.question: Who do I call for emergencies?
questions.answer: Campus Emergency: 911 or (555) 123-4570. Campus Security: (555) 123-4571. Emergency phones located throughout campus.

questions.question: How do I reach housing services?
questions.answer: Housing Office: (555) 123-4572, housing@campus.edu. Located in Harrison Hall, first floor.

questions.question: Who do I contact about parking?
questions.answer: Parking Services: (555) 123-4573, parking@campus.edu. Office in Security Building. Permits available online.

questions.question: What's the health center number?
questions.answer: Health & Wellness Center: (555) 123-4574. Emergency line available 24/7. Appointments: healthcenter@campus.edu.

questions.question: Who handles academic advising?
questions.answer: Academic Advising: (555) 123-4575, advising@campus.edu. Located in each academic department building.

questions.question: How do I register for classes?
questions.answer: Class registration: 1) Meet with advisor, 2) Log into student portal, 3) Select courses, 4) Pay tuition. Registration opens by class level.

questions.question: How do I connect to WiFi?
questions.answer: WiFi: Connect to 'CampusWiFi' network, use your student ID and password. For guests, use 'CampusGuest' (no password required).

questions.question: How do I get a student ID?
questions.answer: Student ID: Visit ID Services in Student Center with photo ID and enrollment verification. Cards ready in 10 minutes. Cost: $20.

questions.question: How do I access the student portal?
questions.answer: Student Portal: Go to portal.campus.edu, use your student ID number and the password you created during orientation.

questions.question: How do I drop a class?
questions.answer: Drop classes: Log into student portal > My Classes > Drop. Deadline varies by semester progress. Consult advisor first.

questions.question: How do I get transcripts?
questions.answer: Transcripts: Order online through student portal or visit Registrar's Office. Official transcripts: $10 each. Unofficial: free online.

questions.question: How do I apply for financial aid?
questions.answer: Financial aid: Complete FAFSA online, submit by March 1 for priority consideration. Visit Financial Aid Office for help.

questions.question: How do I reserve a study room?
questions.answer: Study rooms: Reserve online through library website or use kiosks in library. Maximum 4 hours per day. Free for students.

questions.question: What events are happening today?
questions.answer: Check the campus events calendar at events.campus.edu or visit the Student Center information desk for daily activities.

questions.question: What clubs can I join?
questions.answer: 200+ student organizations available. Visit Student Life Office or attend the Activities Fair each semester. List at studentlife.campus.edu.

questions.question: When is the career fair?
questions.answer: Career Fair: Fall semester (October 15-16), Spring semester (March 10-11). Register through Career Services office.

questions.question: Are there sports events this week?
questions.answer: Check athletics.campus.edu for game schedules. Home games at Campus Stadium. Student tickets free with ID.

questions.question: What study groups are available?
questions.answer: Study groups organized by Academic Success Center. Check with your department or visit tutoring.campus.edu for subject-specific groups.

questions.question: When is orientation?
questions.answer: New student orientation: Multiple dates in August and January. Check admissions.campus.edu for schedules and registration.

questions.question: Hello
questions.answer: Hello! I'm NEXi, your campus assistant. I can help you find locations, get hours, contact information, and learn about campus procedures.

questions.question: Good morning
questions.answer: Good morning! How can I help you navigate campus today?

questions.question: Can you help me?
questions.answer: Absolutely! I'm here to help with campus information. What do you need to know about?

questions.question: Thanks
questions.answer: You're welcome! Feel free to ask me anything else about campus.

questions.question: Goodbye
questions.answer: Goodbye! Have a great day on campus. I'm here whenever you need help!

questions.question: How do I print documents?
questions.answer: Printing: Use campus printers with your student ID. $0.10 per page B&W, $0.25 color. Add money at print stations or online.

questions.question: Where are the computer labs?
questions.answer: Computer labs: Library (24/7), Academic buildings (limited hours), Student Center. All require student ID for access.

questions.question: How do I access campus email?
questions.answer: Campus email: Go to mail.campus.edu, use your student ID and password. Also available on mobile devices through campus email app.

questions.question: What software is available?
questions.answer: Free software for students: Microsoft Office, Adobe Creative Suite, programming tools. Download from software.campus.edu.

questions.question: What meal plans are available?
questions.answer: Meal plans: Unlimited ($2,400/semester), 19 meals/week ($2,200), 14 meals/week ($2,000), 10 meals/week ($1,800). Plus dining dollars included.

questions.question: What food is served today?
questions.answer: Today's menu: Check dining.campus.edu or the Campus Dining app for real-time menus and nutritional information.

questions.question: Are there vegetarian options?
questions.answer: Vegetarian/vegan options available daily at all dining locations. Special dietary needs accommodated - contact Dining Services.

questions.question: How do I schedule a doctor appointment?
questions.answer: Health appointments: Call (555) 123-4574, use the health portal online, or visit Health Center. Same-day sick visits available.

questions.question: Is counseling available?
questions.answer: Counseling services: Free for students. Call (555) 123-4574 or visit health.campus.edu. Crisis counseling available 24/7.

questions.question: Where is the pharmacy?
questions.answer: Campus pharmacy located in Health Center. Prescription refills, over-the-counter medications. Student insurance accepted.

questions.question: Where is tutoring available?
questions.answer: Tutoring: Academic Success Center (Library 2nd floor), department-specific tutoring in academic buildings. Free for students.

questions.question: How do I change my major?
questions.answer: Change major: Meet with current advisor, visit new department advisor, complete change of major form in Registrar's Office.

questions.question: When is registration?
questions.answer: Registration dates vary by class level. Check student portal for your specific registration time. Seniors register first.

questions.question: How do I get academic accommodations?
questions.answer: Academic accommodations: Visit Disability Services in Student Center with documentation. They coordinate with professors.
